title: Close the highest-value Hermes parity gaps in pk-pi-hermes-evolve
objective: Move pk-pi-hermes-evolve from a good-faith Hermes-inspired adaptation toward a stronger pi-native Hermes Phase 1 analogue. Prioritize execution-based evaluation, structured traces, richer dataset splits, and validation hooks without abandoning the current review-first safety model.
acceptable_end_state: The repository contains a traced, retryable Ralph loop that can drive this parity work; the loop writes JSON step artifacts and OpenTelemetry spans, runs real repo validation commands after each step, and can judge whether more work is needed. The package also remains typecheck-clean and Python-syntax-clean after the loop's edits.
deliverables: A runnable scripts/ralph_otel.py orchestration loop tailored to this repository
A task spec that captures the Hermes parity objective for the loop
Python backend dependency metadata for OpenTelemetry exporters
README documentation for running the Ralph loop against this repo
Validation coverage in the loop for TypeScript typecheck and Python syntax checks
success_signals: The Ralph loop emits a root span named ralph.run/<task>
Each loop iteration emits loop.step, model.infer, tool, and judge spans
Logs include correlated trace and span identifiers
The loop can stop early when the judge passes or when the worker reports no more steps are needed
out_of_scope: Republishing the npm package during this implementation step
Implementing full Hermes benchmark parity in the same change
Autonomous direct writes to the target artifact without human review

repo_checks.description: The current Ralph run writes a summary artifact.
The current Ralph run writes per-step JSON artifacts.
The Ralph loop code contains the expected traced span names.
TypeScript orchestration exposes a validation split in addition to train and holdout.
Python backend exposes a validation split in addition to train and holdout.
TypeScript codebase mentions or implements golden dataset support.
Python backend mentions or implements golden dataset support.

repo_notes: The project is a pi-native extension with a TypeScript orchestrator and optional Python backend under python_backend/.
Current known Hermes parity gaps include execution-based evaluation, trace capture, validation split support, golden datasets, and benchmark/test gates.
The Ralph loop should help close those gaps rather than replace the existing extension entrypoints.
